given: the SKU detail page is rendered for a SKU with stock at multiple locations, and those records have batch_number and serial_number populated
then: each location row displays batch number and serial number as separate labeled fields
architectural_notes: E2E: drives the SKU detail view UI across the full read path for a SKU with stock at multiple locations. Boundary (app/routes/) renders the detail template; per-location batch/serial values flow boundary -> service (app/services/) -> repository (app/repositories/) -> models (app/models/stock.py), reading the split fields directly. Cross-cutting concern: the labeled-field layout is a presentation concern owned by the boundary + template; the detail read reflects every committed location row without bypassing the repository.

independence.rationale: AC1-AC2 test the home table; AC3 tests the distinct SKU detail view with a different UI structure (labeled fields instead of columns)
